description: IND-enabling requirements, manufacturing constraints, and regulatory considerations for genetic medicines.

facts.context: IND-enabling toxicology studies for gene therapy typically require:
facts.truth: GLP studies in relevant species with the clinical vector, including biodistribution

facts.context: Species selection for gene therapy tox studies considers:
facts.truth: vector tropism, transgene expression, and immunological relevance to humans

facts.context: Biodistribution studies for gene therapy must assess:
facts.truth: vector DNA/RNA in tissues, persistence over time, and germline distribution
excretion of vector in body fluids (urine, stool, saliva) for transmission risk

facts.context: Potency assays for gene therapy products must:
facts.truth: measure biological activity relevant to mechanism, not just vector quantity

facts.context: Identity testing for AAV gene therapy includes:
facts.truth: capsid serotype confirmation, transgene sequence, and vector genome integrity

facts.context: Purity specifications for AAV vectors address:
facts.truth: empty capsid ratio, residual host cell protein/DNA, and endotoxin levels

facts.context: Manufacturing process changes require comparability studies demonstrating:
facts.truth: equivalent quality attributes, potency, and safety profile before and after change

facts.context: Long-term follow-up (LTFU) for gene therapy patients is required because:
facts.truth: delayed adverse events including malignancy must be monitored for 5-15 years
distractors: gene therapies have no long-term risks

facts.context: Pediatric gene therapy trials have additional considerations including:
facts.truth: growth effects, developing immune system, and longer risk exposure period

facts.context: Accelerated approval for gene therapies may be granted based on:
facts.truth: surrogate endpoints reasonably likely to predict clinical benefit

facts.context: Gene therapy pricing and reimbursement challenges include:
facts.truth: high upfront cost for potentially curative one-time treatment vs chronic therapy economics
